summary: Mobile responsiveness testing for TopNav hamburger menu, Admin sidebar toggle, Store filters/hero/search, admin tables scroll, and desktop smoke test at 1280px. All 8 critical tests passed.

issues: Search input has fixed narrow width (160px) while sort dropdown takes full width (358px) on mobile. The visual asymmetry is minor — not a bug but slightly imbalanced. Could consider making search input flex-1 on small screens.

test_results_detail.test1_topnav_hamburger_390px: PASS - Hamburger visible, desktop nav hidden, mobile drawer opens/closes correctly with 4 nav links
test_results_detail.test2_topnav_desktop_1280px: PASS - Hamburger hidden, desktop nav links visible, user menu trigger visible
test_results_detail.test3_admin_mobile_sidebar_390px: PASS - Sidebar toggle button visible, backdrop overlay appears on click, sidebar opens showing all nav tabs, closes after tab selection (confirmed visually via screenshots)
test_results_detail.test4_store_mobile_filters_390px: PASS - Mobile filters toggle visible, category sidebar hidden by default, opens/closes correctly
test_results_detail.test5_store_hero_overflow_390px: PASS - Hero width=358px, right edge=374px, no overflow. Page scroll width = viewport (390px)
test_results_detail.test6_store_search_sort_stacking_390px: PASS - Search at y=445 (width=160px), sort at y=489 (width=358px), stacked vertically, no overflow
test_results_detail.test7_admin_tables_mobile_scroll_390px: PASS - Table is 1113px wide inside scroll container (overflow-x: auto). Page scroll width = 390px (no page overflow)
test_results_detail.test8_desktop_smoke_1280px: PASS - Store hero visible, layout visible, category sidebar visible, mobile filters toggle hidden. Admin page visible, tabs visible, sidebar toggle hidden

critical_code_review_comments: All implementations look correct. The CSS media query rules at line 337-366 in index.css properly handle mobile table overflow and store layout stacking.
Admin sidebar uses translate-x-0/-translate-x-full CSS transitions with z-index layering — works correctly.
Store filters use conditional hidden/block class logic — works correctly.
TopNav hamburger uses md:hidden/hidden classes — works correctly.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile responsiveness testing was completed successfully. All 8 tests pass. Auth via POST /api/auth/login with admin@automateaccounts.local/ChangeMe123! and set 'aa_token' in localStorage. Test at 390px (iPhone 14) and 1280px viewports. Key test IDs: nav-hamburger, nav-mobile-menu, admin-sidebar-toggle, mobile-filters-toggle, category-sidebar, store-hero, store-layout. All mobile optimizations are working correctly.
